Game Drama Score.details: Decided by 9 points with 14 lead changes and 38 ties. The game saw 2 major win probability swings, and the losing team's chances peaked at 27%.

Most Valuable Play.details: Q1, 8:59, Hartenstein (OKC): Hartenstein Offensive Foul Turnover (P1.T1). It swung the win probability by +6.2 points.

Most Damaging Play.details: Q1, 2:17, Bryant (SAS): Bryant Bad Pass Turnover (P1.T3). It swung the win probability by -8.8 points.

Clutch-Time Scoring Summary.value: Scoring over the final five minutes: home team 10, away team 8.

recap.Biggest Turning Point: The game turned at Q3, 7:18: Caruso 26' 3PT Jump Shot (10 PTS) (Gilgeous-Alexander 7 AST). That single play moved OKC's chance of winning from 54% to 74%. No other moment shifted the game more, and it went OKC's way.
recap.Player Impact: No one influenced the outcome more than Gilgeous-Alexander of OKC. Across 46 plays, his actions moved the win probability by a combined 91 percentage points, the most of any player on the floor.
recap.Comeback Reality: The tensest moment for a comeback came at Q1, 2:35, with OKC down 5 points. The model put their chance of pulling it off at 36%. In other words: possible, but not nothing.
recap.Hidden Momentum: The strongest run that didn't fully show on the scoreboard came at Q1, 1:16, with the game tilting OKC's way. The play in the middle of it: Wallace 3PT Jump Shot (3 PTS) (Gilgeous-Alexander 2 AST).
recap.Model Note: These numbers come from ClutchCast's champion model, the winner of a competition between six models. The winner is judged on how honest its probabilities are on games it never trained on, not just how often it picks the right winner.
